NKJV.1: Now the Spirit expressly says that in latter times some will depart from the faith, giving heed to deceiving spirits and doctrines of demons,
NKJV.2: speaking lies in hypocrisy, having their own conscience seared with a hot iron,
NKJV.3: forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
NKJV.4: For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be refused if it is received with thanksgiving;
NKJV.5: for it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
NKJV.6: If you instruct the brethren in these things, you will be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished in the words of faith and of the good doctrine which you have carefully followed.
NKJV.7: But reject profane and old wives’ fables, and exercise yourself toward godliness.
NKJV.8: For bodily exercise profits a little, but godliness is profitable for all things, having promise of the life that now is and of that which is to come.
NKJV.9: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptance.
NKJV.10: For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.
NKJV.12: Let no one despise your youth, but be an example to the believers in word, in conduct, in love, in spirit, in faith, in purity.
NKJV.13: Till I come, give attention to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.
NKJV.14: Do not neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy with the laying on of the hands of the eldership.
NKJV.15: Meditate on these things; give yourself entirely to them, that your progress may be evident to all.
NKJV.16: Take heed to yourself and to the doctrine. Continue in them, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.

ESV.1: Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will depart from the faith by devoting themselves to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons,
ESV.2: through the insincerity of liars whose consciences are seared,
ESV.3: who forbid marriage and require abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
ESV.4: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving,
ESV.5: for it is made holy by the word of God and prayer.
ESV.6: If you put these things before the brothers,  you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, being trained in the words of the faith and of the good doctrine that you have followed.
ESV.7: Have nothing to do with irreverent, silly myths. Rather train yourself for godliness;
ESV.8: for while bodily training is of some value, godliness is of value in every way, as it holds promise for the present life and also for the life to come.
ESV.9: The saying is trustworthy and deserving of full acceptance.
ESV.10: For to this end we toil and strive,  because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.
ESV.12: Let no one despise you for your youth, but set the believers an example in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.
ESV.13: Until I come, devote yourself to the public reading of Scripture, to exhortation, to teaching.
ESV.14: Do not neglect the gift you have, which was given you by prophecy when the council of elders laid their hands on you.
ESV.15: Practice these things, immerse yourself in them,  so that all may see your progress.
ESV.16: Keep a close watch on yourself and on the teaching. Persist in this, for by so doing you will save both yourself and your hearers.

NASB.1: But the Spirit explicitly says that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to deceitful spirits and doctrines of demons,
NASB.2: by means of the hypocrisy of liars seared in their own conscience as with a branding iron,
NASB.3: [men] who forbid marriage [and advocate] abstaining from foods which God has created to be gratefully shared in by those who believe and know the truth.
NASB.4: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with gratitude;
NASB.5: for it is sanctified by means of the word of God and prayer.
NASB.6: In pointing out these things to the brethren, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, [constantly] nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound doctrine which you have been following.
NASB.7: But have nothing to do with worldly fables fit only for old women. On the other hand, discipline yourself for the purpose of godliness;
NASB.8: for bodily discipline is only of little profit, but godliness is profitable for all things, since it holds promise for the present life and [also] for the [life] to come.
NASB.9: It is a trustworthy statement deserving full acceptance.
NASB.10: For it is for this we labor and strive, because we have fixed our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of believers.
NASB.12: Let no one look down on your youthfulness, but [rather] in speech, conduct, love, faith [and] purity, show yourself an example of those who believe.
NASB.13: Until I come, give attention to the [public] reading [of Scripture], to exhortation and teaching.
NASB.14: Do not neglect the spiritual gift within you, which was bestowed on you through prophetic utterance with the laying on of hands by the presbytery.
NASB.15: Take pains with these things; be [absorbed] in them, so that your progress will be evident to all.
NASB.16: Pay close attention to yourself and to your teaching; persevere in these things, for as you do this you will ensure salvation both for yourself and for those who hear you.

NLT.1: Now the Holy Spirit tells us clearly that in the last times some will turn away from the true faith; they will follow deceptive spirits and teachings that come from demons.
NLT.2: These people are hypocrites and liars, and their consciences are dead.
NLT.3: They will say it is wrong to be married and wrong to eat certain foods. But God created those foods to be eaten with thanks by faithful people who know the truth.
NLT.4: Since everything God created is good, we should not reject any of it but receive it with thanks.
NLT.5: For we know it is made acceptable by the word of God and prayer.
NLT.6: If you explain these things to the brothers and sisters, Timothy, you will be a worthy servant of Christ Jesus, one who is nourished by the message of faith and the good teaching you have followed.
NLT.7: Do not waste time arguing over godless ideas and old wives’ tales. Instead, train yourself to be godly.
NLT.8: “Physical training is good, but training for godliness is much better, promising benefits in this life and in the life to come.”
NLT.9: This is a trustworthy saying, and everyone should accept it.
NLT.10: This is why we work hard and continue to struggle, for our hope is in the living God, who is the Savior of all people and particularly of all believers.
NLT.11: Teach these things and insist that everyone learn them.
NLT.12: Don’t let anyone think less of you because you are young. Be an example to all believers in what you say, in the way you live, in your love, your faith, and your purity.
NLT.13: Until I get there, focus on reading the Scriptures to the church, encouraging the believers, and teaching them.
NLT.14: Do not neglect the spiritual gift you received through the prophecy spoken over you when the elders of the church laid their hands on you.
NLT.15: Give your complete attention to these matters. Throw yourself into your tasks so that everyone will see your progress.
NLT.16: Keep a close watch on how you live and on your teaching. Stay true to what is right for the sake of your own salvation and the salvation of those who hear you.

WEB.1: But the Spirit says expressly that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to seducing spirits and doctrines of demons,
WEB.2: through the hypocrisy of men who speak lies, branded in their own conscience as with a hot iron;
WEB.3: forbidding marriage and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
WEB.4: For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, if it is received with thanksgiving.
WEB.5: For it is sanctified through the word of God and prayer.
WEB.6: If you instruct the brothers of these things, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished in the words of the faith, and of the good doctrine which you have followed.
WEB.7: But refuse profane and old wives’ fables. Exercise yourself toward godliness.
WEB.8: For bodily exercise has some value, but godliness has value in all things, having the promise of the life which is now, and of that which is to come.
WEB.9: This saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance.
WEB.10: For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we have set our trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.
WEB.12: Let no man despise your youth; but be an example to those who believe, in word, in your way of life, in love, in spirit, in faith, and in purity.
WEB.13: Until I come, pay attention to reading, to exhortation, and to teaching.
WEB.14: Don’t neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the elders.
WEB.15: Be diligent in these things. Give yourself wholly to them, that your progress may be revealed to all.
WEB.16: Pay attention to yourself, and to your teaching. Continue in these things, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.

CSB17.1: Now the Spirit <sup>ⓒ</sup> explicitly says that in later times <sup>ⓓ</sup> some will depart from the faith, <sup>ⓔ</sup> paying attention to deceitful spirits <sup>ⓕ</sup> and the teachings of demons, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.2: through the hypocrisy <sup>ⓗ</sup> of liars whose consciences <sup>ⓘ</sup> are seared.
CSB17.3: They forbid marriage <sup>ⓙ</sup> and demand abstinence <sup>ⓚ</sup> from foods that God created <sup>ⓛ</sup> to be received with gratitude by those who believe and know the truth.
CSB17.4: For everything created by God is good, <sup>ⓜ</sup> and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving, <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.5: since it is sanctified by the word of God <sup>ⓐ</sup> and by prayer.
CSB17.6: If you point these things out to the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished by the words of the faith <sup>ⓑ</sup> and the good teaching that you have followed. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.7: But have nothing to do with pointless and silly myths. <sup>ⓓ</sup> Rather, train yourself in godliness. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.8: For the training of the body has limited benefit, <sup>ⓕ</sup> but godliness is beneficial in every way, since it holds promise for the present life and also for the life to come. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.9: This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.10: For this reason we labor and strive, <sup>[1]</sup> because we have put our hope in the living God, <sup>ⓘ</sup> who is the Savior <sup>ⓙ</sup> of all people, <sup>ⓚ</sup> especially of those who believe.
CSB17.12: Don’t let anyone despise your youth, but set an example for the believers in speech, <sup>ⓛ</sup> in conduct, <sup>ⓜ</sup> in love, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> in faith, and in purity. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.13: Until I come, <sup>ⓑ</sup> give your attention to public reading, exhortation, and teaching.
CSB17.14: Don’t neglect the gift that is in you; it was given to you through prophecy, <sup>ⓒ</sup> with the laying on of hands <sup>ⓓ</sup> by the council of elders.
CSB17.15: Practice these things; be committed to them, so that your progress may be evident to all.
CSB17.16: Pay close attention to your life and your teaching; persevere in these things, for in doing this you will save <sup>ⓔ</sup> both yourself and your hearers. <sup>ⓕ</sup>

AMP.1: But the [Holy] Spirit explicitly and unmistakably declares that in later times some will turn away from the faith, paying attention instead to deceitful and seductive spirits and doctrines of demons,
AMP.2: [misled] by the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared as with a branding iron [leaving them incapable of ethical functioning],
AMP.3: who forbid marriage and advocate abstaining from [certain kinds of] foods which God has created to be gratefully shared by those who believe and have [a clear] knowledge of the truth.
AMP.4: For everything God has created is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with gratitude;
AMP.5: for it is sanctified [set apart, dedicated to God] by means of the word of God and prayer.
AMP.6: If you point out these instructions to the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, constantly nourished [through study] on the words of the faith and of the good [Christian] doctrine which you have closely followed.
AMP.7: But have nothing to do with irreverent folklore and silly myths. On the other hand, discipline yourself for the purpose of godliness [keeping yourself spiritually fit].
AMP.8: For physical training is of some value, but godliness (spiritual training) is of value in everything and in every way, since it holds promise for the present life and for the life to come.
AMP.9: This is a faithful and trustworthy saying worthy of full acceptance and approval.
AMP.10: It is for this that we labor and strive [often called to account], because we have fixed our [confident] hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe [in Him, recognize Him as the Son of God, and accept Him as Savior and Lord].
AMP.11: Keep commanding and teaching these things.
AMP.12: Let no one look down on [you because of] your youth, but be an example and set a pattern for the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, and in [moral] purity.
AMP.13: Until I come, devote yourself to public reading [of Scripture], to preaching and to teaching [the sound doctrine of God's word].
AMP.14: Do not neglect the spiritual gift within you, [that special endowment] which was intentionally bestowed on you [by the Holy Spirit] through prophetic utterance when the elders laid their hands on you [at your ordination].
AMP.15: Practice and work hard on these things; be absorbed in them [completely occupied in your ministry], so that your progress will be evident to all.
AMP.16: Pay close attention to yourself [concentrate on your personal development] and to your teaching; persevere in these things [hold to them], for as you do this you will ensure salvation both for yourself and for those who hear you.

MSG.1: The Spirit makes it clear that as time goes on, some are going to give up on the faith and chase after demonic illusions put forth by professional liars.
MSG.2: These liars have lied so well and for so long that they've lost their capacity for truth.
MSG.3: They will tell you not to get married. They'll tell you not to eat this or that food—perfectly good food God created to be eaten heartily and with thanksgiving by Christians!
MSG.4: Everything God created is good, and to be received with thanks. Nothing is to be sneered at and thrown out.
MSG.5: God's Word and our prayers make every item in creation holy.
MSG.6: You've been raised on the Message of the faith and have followed sound teaching. Now pass on this counsel to the Christians there, and you'll be a good servant of Jesus.
MSG.7: Stay clear of silly stories that get dressed up as religion. Exercise daily in God—no spiritual flabbiness, please!
MSG.8: Workouts in the gymnasium are useful, but a disciplined life in God is far more so, making you fit both today and forever.
MSG.9: You can count on this. Take it to heart.
MSG.10: This is why we've thrown ourselves into this venture so totally. We're banking on the living God, Savior of all men and women, especially believers.
MSG.11: Get the word out. Teach all these things.
MSG.12: And don't let anyone put you down because you're young. Teach believers with your life: by word, by demeanor, by love, by faith, by integrity.
MSG.13: Stay at your post reading Scripture, giving counsel, teaching.
MSG.14: And that special gift of ministry you were given when the leaders of the church laid hands on you and prayed—keep that dusted off and in use.
MSG.15: Cultivate these things. Immerse yourself in them. The people will all see you mature right before their eyes!
MSG.16: Keep a firm grasp on both your character and your teaching. Don't be diverted. Just keep at it. Both you and those who hear you will experience salvation.

NIV.1:
Instructions to Timothy
The Spirit clearly says that in later times some will abandon the faith and follow deceiving spirits and things taught by demons.
NIV.2: Such teachings come through hypocritical liars, whose consciences have been seared as with a hot iron.
NIV.3: They forbid people to marry and order them to abstain from certain foods, which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and who know the truth.
NIV.4: For everything God created is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving,
NIV.5: because it is consecrated by the word of God and prayer.
NIV.6: If you point these things out to the brothers, you will be a good minister of Christ Jesus, brought up in the truths of the faith and of the good teaching that you have followed.
NIV.7: Have nothing to do with godless myths and old wives’ tales; rather, train yourself to be godly.
NIV.8: For physical training is of some value, but godliness has value for all things, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.
NIV.9: This is a trustworthy saying that deserves full acceptance
NIV.10: (and for this we labor and strive), that we have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, and especially of those who believe.
NIV.12: Don’t let anyone look down on you because you are young, but set an example for the believers in speech, in life, in love, in faith and in purity.
NIV.13: Until I come, devote yourself to the public reading of Scripture, to preaching and to teaching.
NIV.14: Do not neglect your gift, which was given you through a prophetic message when the body of elders laid their hands on you.
NIV.15: Be diligent in these matters; give yourself wholly to them, so that everyone may see your progress.
NIV.16: Watch your life and doctrine closely. Persevere in them, because if you do, you will save both yourself and your hearers.

NRSVCE.1: Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will renounce the faith by paying attention to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons,
NRSVCE.2: through the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared with a hot iron.
NRSVCE.3: They forbid marriage and demand abstinence from foods, which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
NRSVCE.4: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, provided it is received with thanksgiving;
NRSVCE.5: for it is sanctified by God’s word and by prayer.
NRSVCE.6: If you put these instructions before the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound teaching that you have followed.
NRSVCE.7: Have nothing to do with profane myths and old wives’ tales. Train yourself in godliness,
NRSVCE.8: for, while physical training is of some value, godliness is valuable in every way, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.
NRSVCE.9: The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance.
NRSVCE.10: For to this end we toil and struggle, because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.
NRSVCE.11: These are the things you must insist on and teach.
NRSVCE.12: Let no one despise your youth, but set the believers an example in speech and conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.
NRSVCE.13: Until I arrive, give attention to the public reading of scripture, to exhorting, to teaching.
NRSVCE.14: Do not neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you through prophecy with the laying on of hands by the council of elders.
NRSVCE.15: Put these things into practice, devote yourself to them, so that all may see your progress.
NRSVCE.16: Pay close attention to yourself and to your teaching; continue in these things, for in doing this you will save both yourself and your hearers.

NET.1: Now the Spirit explicitly says that in the later times some will desert the faith and occupy themselves with deceiving spirits and demonic teachings,
NET.2: influenced by the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared.
NET.3: They will prohibit marriage and require abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
NET.4: For every creation of God is good and no food is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving.
NET.5: For it is sanctified by God’s word and by prayer.
NET.6: By pointing out such things to the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, having nourished yourself on the words of the faith and of the good teaching that you have followed.
NET.7: But reject those myths fit only for the godless and gullible, and train yourself for godliness.
NET.8: For “physical exercise has some value, but godliness is valuable in every way. It holds promise for the present life and for the life to come.”
NET.9: This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance.
NET.10: In fact this is why we work hard and struggle, because we have set our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of believers.
NET.12: Let no one look down on you because you are young, but set an example for the believers in your speech, conduct, love, faithfulness, and purity.
NET.13: Until I come, give attention to the public reading of scripture, to exhortation, to teaching.
NET.14: Do not neglect the spiritual gift you have, given to you and confirmed by prophetic words when the elders laid hands on you.
NET.15: Take pains with these things; be absorbed in them, so that everyone will see your progress.
NET.16: Be conscientious about how you live and what you teach. Persevere in this, because by doing so you will save both yourself and those who listen to you.
